user_query:
I’m preparing a trends digest for our China market team, but I need you to work offline from a provided snapshot. Please read both input/baidu_hot_snapshot.json (a single JSON object with a "data" array of hot topics, each having rank, title, category, and search_count) and input/keywords.txt (one keyword per line). Using only these two files (no external calls), please:

1) Analyze the snapshot and compute:
- Total item count.
- Category distribution counts.
- Top 12 items by ascending rank.
- Case-insensitive keyword tracking: for each keyword, how many titles matched and which ranks matched.
- “Rising” items defined as category == "New" (count these and list their ranks).
- Percent share per item = (item.search_count / sum of all search_count) * 100, rounded to one decimal place.
- Top 3 items by percent share (titles only).

2) Write all results to the following files under output/:
- output/summary.json with this structure:
  {
    "generated_at": ISO8601 string,
    "total_items": number,
    "categories": { "<category>": count, ... },
    "top_12": [ { "rank": number, "title": string, "category": string, "search_count": number, "percent_share": number }, ... exactly 12 items ordered by ascending rank ],
    "keyword_hits": { "ai": {"count": number, "ranks": [numbers...]}, "315": {"count": number, "ranks": [...]}, "football": {"count": number, "ranks": [...]}, "chicken feet": {"count": number, "ranks": [...] } },
    "rising": { "count": number, "ranks": [numbers...] },
    "top3_by_share": [string, string, string]
  }
- output/tracked.jsonl where each line is a JSON object for any title that matched at least one keyword, with fields: { "rank": number, "title": string, "category": string, "search_count": number, "matched_keywords": [lowercased keywords that matched, unique and sorted] }.
- output/report.html containing a readable summary with an H1 "Baidu Hot Topics Report", a section listing each keyword and the matched titles (if any), and a table that lists the top 12 items and their percent_share.
- output/dataset.csv with header exactly: rank,title,category,search_count,percent_share and one row for each of the top 12 items, ordered by ascending rank.

3) Implementation rules:
- Do not call any external services; operate only on the provided files.
- Treat keyword matching as case-insensitive substring search on the title.
- Use only relative paths under input/ and output/.
- Ensure all files are validly formatted (JSON parses, HTML contains a <table>, CSV header matches exactly).

Let me know if anything in the snapshot format is unclear and write the four files under output/ exactly as specified.
